body:
## Summary
- When an issue event reports a weak author association, keep the existing issue-author-association refresh and add a collaborator endpoint check for the issue author.
- Promote issue authors to COLLABORATOR only when GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/collaborators/{username} succeeds, avoiding public-read users from being trusted.
- Document the fallback and add regression coverage for the collaborator promotion path.

## Stacked on
- #12

## Verification
- npm --prefix .agent ci
- npm --prefix .agent test

comments.body:
## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 100 | PASS | 5 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow / generic | Reuse existing code | pass | 8/8 | PR extends existing extraction/auth flow in [`extract-context.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/582bc249533d79072978fc3e7093af03c777276b/.agent/src/cli/extract-context.ts) and reuses existing GitHub helpers (`ghApiOk`) instead of introducing parallel logic paths. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 8/8 | Diff is tightly scoped to 3 directly relevant files only: runtime logic, targeted tests, and access-policy docs ([PR files](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/13/files)). |
| coding_workflow / generic | Keep docs in sync | pass | 7/7 | Behavior change (collaborator fallback) is documented in [`access-policy.md`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/582bc249533d79072978fc3e7093af03c777276b/.agent/docs/access-policy.md). |
| coding_style / generic | Prefer simple implementations | pass | 7/7 | Implementation adds a minimal collaborator check function and a small extension to `refreshIssueAssociation`, avoiding broader refactors or extra abstraction layers. |
| coding_workflow / generic | No per-file inventory tables in docs | pass | 6/6 | Docs update is principle-based narrative text; it does not add file-by-file inventory content. |

## Notes

- Main score drivers: focused change set, explicit docs update for new runtime behavior, and regression coverage for collaborator promotion in [`extract-context-cli.test.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/582bc249533d79072978fc3e7093af03c777276b/.agent/src/__tests__/extract-context-cli.test.ts).
- Rubrics such as merge strategy, rubric-authoring, and orchestration follow-up defaults are not applicable to this PR’s implementation diff.

## Findings

- **INFO:** No rubric-specific violations found in the current PR diff.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PASS

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
- This PR strengthens trust evaluation for issue-triggered runs when webhook `author_association` is weak (`NONE`/`CONTRIBUTOR`) but the user is actually a repo collaborator.
- It keeps the existing issue refresh step and adds a second check using `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/collaborators/{username}` to promote qualifying authors to `COLLABORATOR`.
- The change is stacked on PR #12 and remains scoped to runtime logic, targeted tests, and access-policy docs.
- Reviewer outputs consistently agree that the trust boundary direction is correct and that behavior remains conservative for non-collaborators.
- Existing PR discussion/inline history does not already contain unresolved line-level issues requiring duplicate comments.

## Review
- Overall judgment: implementation is sound and merge-ready, with only optional INFO-level polish noted by one reviewer. No BLOCKING or WARNING defects were identified across the provided reviews.
- I did not post inline comments because there were no concrete, must-fix line-level issues that warranted direct patch feedback.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| No unresolved actionable issues identified | INFO | Reviews converge on correct behavior, adequate regression coverage, and docs alignment; remaining notes are optional hygiene improvements. |

## Progress
- Collaborator-promotion behavior is already implemented and documented.
- Regression test coverage for collaborator promotion is already added.
- Optional suggestions (extra observability log and an explicit negative-path test) were raised as non-blocking and not required for this PR’s scope.

## Recommended Next Step
- `NO_AUTOMATED_ACTION`: No unresolved actionable branch changes remain; this PR is ready to proceed as-is.

## Final Verdict
- `SHIP`

## Action Items
- [ ] No required branch changes.

## Rubrics Update

No changes were committed to `agent/rubrics` from PR #13.

no rubric changes

Reasoning:
- Reviewed PR #13 metadata, issue comments, review comments, reviews, and diff.
- Conversation sources were bot-authored comments (`sepo-agent-app[bot]`, untrusted for rubric learning) plus the PR author `lolipopshock` with `author_association: CONTRIBUTOR`.
- No trusted maintainer/owner/collaborator human comment in the PR thread gave an explicit durable “future agents should …” preference.
- The PR body content is implementation-specific and does not introduce a new stable rubric beyond existing active rubrics (for example, `keep-docs-in-sync`, `surgical-changes`, `reuse-existing-code`).

So there was no warranted durable rubric to add or update from this PR conversation.

title: Promote issue authors with collaborator access
